senses.definition: a set of ideas and activities involved in governing a country, state, or city

examples.x: After years of studying law and international relations, she decided to enter politics to make meaningful changes in her country.
The mayor's approach to politics focuses on grassroots involvement and transparent decision-making.
Understanding the complex landscape of global politics is crucial for anyone aspiring to work in diplomacy.
Many young people are disillusioned with traditional politics and are seeking new ways to influence their communities.
The professor's lecture on American politics covered the historical evolution of its political parties.

senses.definition: someone who is competing in an election or for a job position

examples.x: The candidate for mayor gave an inspiring speech at the rally.
There are several candidates running for office in the upcoming election.
He is the leading candidate in the race for the university's student president.
After a rigorous interview process, she was selected as the top candidate for the job.
The candidate promised to tackle climate change if elected.

senses.definition: a line that separates two countries, provinces, or states from each other

examples.x: Diplomatic tensions can escalate when disputes arise over the delineation of borders between nations.
Smugglers frequently attempt to cross goods illegally through remote areas along the border.
The fence marks the border between the two properties, ensuring clear demarcation.
As we crossed the border into the neighboring country, we were greeted by customs officials.
The border patrol is responsible for monitoring and enforcing immigration laws along the country's borders.

senses.definition: (in some countries) the group of people who have been elected to make laws, which in the US it consists of the House of Representatives and the Senate
a group of elected people who govern a city, town, etc.

examples.x: She was elected to serve on the local council.
The town council voted against the construction project.
Residents can submit complaints to the council office.
The council proposed new environmental regulations.

senses.definition: (in the US) one of the areas into which a state is divided and has a local government of its own

examples.x: The county fair is an annual event that draws people from all over the region to celebrate agriculture and community.
Our county government provides essential services such as law enforcement, public education, and road maintenance.
The historic courthouse in the center of town serves as the administrative hub for the county.
Local farmers in the county grow a variety of crops, including corn, soybeans, and wheat, contributing to the region's agricultural economy.

senses.definition: the place in which legal proceedings are conducted

examples.x: As a witness in the trial, Sarah was required to testify under oath and answer questions posed by the attorneys in the court.
The court issued a verdict after carefully considering the evidence.
The attorney presented a compelling case in court.

senses.definition: the job, skill, or act of managing the relationships between different countries
the process in which people choose a person or group of people for a position, particularly a political one, through voting

examples.x: The upcoming election will determine the next leader of our country, shaping the direction of government policies and priorities.
Election campaigns often involve candidates presenting their platforms and engaging with voters through debates, rallies, and media outreach.
Election results are announced after votes are counted, and winners are declared based on the majority or plurality of votes received.
The country held presidential elections every four years.
Voters lined up early to cast their ballots in the local elections.

senses.definition: to choose a person for a specific job, particularly a political one, by voting

examples.x: Citizens will elect a new mayor in the upcoming municipal elections.
The nation collectively decided to elect the candidate who promised positive change.
Voters have the responsibility to elect representatives who align with their values.
Over the years, voters in the region have elected leaders with diverse backgrounds.
The citizens of the country are electing new leaders who will shape the future.

senses.definition: a group of people who officially represent their government in another country
the group of politicians in control of a country or state

examples.x: The government implemented new policies to improve the country's healthcare system and make it more accessible to all citizens.
After the election, the new government promised to focus on reducing unemployment and increasing economic growth.
The government is responsible for ensuring that laws are followed and citizens' rights are protected.
The government provided disaster relief funds to help communities affected by the recent flood.
In a democratic system, the government is chosen by the people through free and fair elections.

senses.definition: the government of a city or town and not of a country
someone who is in charge of a region or town
a country's rules that all of its citizens are required to obey

examples.x: Breaking the law can result in serious consequences.
The police officer explained the traffic laws to the new driver.
They studied constitutional law in law school.
The company must comply with employment laws when hiring new employees.
It's important to know your rights under the law.

senses.definition: someone who is elected to be the head of a town or city

examples.x: The mayor announced new plans for public transportation.
She was elected as the first female mayor of the city.
The mayor attended the opening ceremony of the new park.
He criticized the mayor for failing to address traffic issues.
A new mayor will be chosen in the upcoming election.

senses.definition: the group of elected representatives whose responsibility is to create, amend, and discuss laws or address political matters

examples.x: The parliament convened to debate the proposed healthcare reform bill.
The prime minister addressed parliament on the country's economic challenges.
The opposition party criticized the government's policies during the parliament meeting.

senses.definition: an official political group with shared beliefs, goals, and policies aiming to be a part of or form a government

examples.x: The political party held a rally to mobilize supporters and promote its platform ahead of the upcoming election.
Members of the party worked tirelessly to canvass neighborhoods and garner votes for their candidates.
After winning a majority in the legislature, the party introduced sweeping reforms to address pressing societal issues.
In celebration of their electoral victory, the party hosted a gala event to thank volunteers and donors for their contributions.

senses.definition: related to or involving the governance of a country or territory

examples.x: Political debates often revolve around issues such as taxation, healthcare, and national security.
The political landscape of the country shifted dramatically after the election.
The media plays a crucial role in informing the public about political developments and holding elected officials accountable.

senses.definition: the leader of a country that has no king or queen

examples.x: The president addressed the nation in a televised speech.
She was sworn in as the first female president of the country.
The president signed the new legislation into law.
He campaigned vigorously to become the next president of the United States.
The president's term in office lasts for four years.

senses.definition: a society's ordinary people

examples.x: The politician promised to serve the interests of the public.
Media coverage plays a significant role in informing the public.
The company's mission is to ensure that its products are accessible to the general public.
The public was outraged by the increase in taxes.
The initiative aims to educate the public about environmental conservation.

senses.definition: the act of making someone suffer because they have done something illegal or wrong

examples.x: The judge handed down a severe punishment to the convicted criminal, sentencing them to several years in prison.
Effective parenting involves using punishment as a means to teach children about responsibility and consequences for their actions.
The school implemented a new policy of restorative justice, focusing on rehabilitation rather than punishment for student misbehavior.
Punishment alone may not always deter future criminal behavior; rehabilitation programs are often necessary to address underlying issues and prevent recidivism.
He accepted his punishment without complaint.

senses.definition: a thing that someone is legally, officially, or morally allowed to do or have

examples.x: Everyone has the right to free speech.
She fought for her right to receive equal pay for equal work.
Citizens are granted the right to a fair trial under the law.

senses.definition: (of law enforcement agencies) to take a person away because they believe that they have done something illegal

examples.x: The police officer decided to arrest the suspect after gathering sufficient evidence.
Law enforcement agencies may arrest individuals suspected of drug trafficking.
The authorities have the power to arrest those who are caught in the act of committing a crime.
Last year, the detective unexpectedly arrested the suspect after a thorough investigation.
Authorities are currently arresting suspects at the scene of the crime.

senses.definition: to officially forbid a particular action, item, or practice

examples.x: Certain fishing practices were banned to protect endangered marine species.
The social media platform implemented a new policy to ban hate speech and discriminatory content.
The company decided to ban smoking in all of its office buildings to promote a healthier work environment.
In an effort to combat illegal fishing practices, the government banned the use of drift nets in its territorial waters.
The international community came together to ban the trade of ivory.

senses.definition: one of the political areas with limited law-making abilities that together form a federal country, like those of Germany or the US

examples.x: California is the most populous state in the United States, known for its diverse culture and economy.
In Germany, each state, or "Bundesland," has its own government and legislative assembly, with authority over certain regional matters.
Uttar Pradesh is the largest state in India by population, with a rich history and cultural heritage.

senses.definition: to do a particular thing that is unlawful or wrong

examples.x: He was arrested for attempting to commit fraud by falsifying financial documents.
The suspect was accused of committing a burglary by breaking into several homes in the neighborhood.
She admitted to committing perjury during the trial by providing false testimony under oath.
He was found guilty of committing embezzlement, misusing company funds for personal gain.
The hacker was apprehended for committing cybercrimes, including unauthorized access to sensitive information.

senses.definition: to get away from captivity

examples.x: The prisoners are escaping through a tunnel they've been digging.

senses.definition: to try to find the truth about a crime, accident, etc. by carefully examining its facts

examples.x: The police were called to investigate the suspicious death.
The company hired a private firm to investigate the allegations of fraud.
She was determined to investigate the incident until she uncovered the truth.
Authorities are working to investigate the source of the contamination.

senses.definition: to unlawfully and intentionally kill another human being

examples.x: The prosecutor accused the defendant of plotting to murder the victim.
In the court trial, the evidence pointed to the defendant's intention to murder.
The suspect was arrested and charged with attempting to murder a fellow inmate.
If the crime goes unsolved, the killer will likely murder again.
Last year, the criminal unexpectedly murdered an innocent bystander.

senses.definition: to cause someone suffering for breaking the law or having done something they should not have

examples.x: The court decided to punish the thief with a prison sentence for stealing.
Parents may choose to punish their children by restricting privileges when rules are violated.
Speeding drivers are often punished with fines to discourage traffic violations.
Schools may punish students for plagiarism by giving them a failing grade on the assignment.
Legal systems have various ways to punish individuals who engage in criminal activities, including imprisonment and fines.
Company policies typically outline consequences to punish employees for unethical behavior in the workplace.

senses.definition: to control and be in charge of a country

examples.x: The monarch ruled the kingdom with absolute authority.
The president ruled the nation during a time of economic prosperity.
The dictator ruled the country with an iron fist, suppressing dissent.
The military junta ruled the nation after a coup d'état.

senses.definition: someone who steals something from a person or place without using violence or threats

examples.x: The thief was caught on camera as he sneaked into the store and took a handful of electronics.
The thief was able to break into the house while the family was away on vacation.
After years of stealing from his coworkers, the thief was finally exposed and fired from his job.
She was horrified when she realized the thief had taken her purse while she was distracted at the café.
The thief attempted to escape through the alley, but the police quickly cornered him.

senses.definition: to show which candidate one wants to win in an election or which plan one supports, by marking a piece of paper, raising one's hand, etc.

examples.x: People often vote for their favorite contestants in reality shows.
Yesterday, she enthusiastically voted for her preferred candidate.
He voted for the first time after turning eighteen.

senses.definition: an official meeting where a group of people discuss a certain matter, which often continues for days

examples.x: The annual tech conference attracts thousands of professionals from around the world.
She presented her research at a scientific conference last week.
The company is hosting a conference to launch its new product line.
He attended a three-day conference on climate change in Berlin.
Many universities organize conferences to promote academic collaboration.

senses.definition: an official announcement regarding something specific
a piece of cloth with a mark or pattern that stands for a country, organization, etc.

examples.x: The American flag was raised during the ceremony.
Each country’s flag was displayed at the international summit.
The UN flag flew outside the headquarters.
They waved the team’s flag in support at the stadium.
The flag of the charity organization was prominently featured in the parade.

senses.definition: an amount of money given by law to the close relatives of a murdered victim
to release someone from captivity or arrest

examples.x: The police decided to free the suspect due to a lack of evidence.
After serving the sentence, the authorities agreed to free the prisoner.
The captors finally chose to free the hostages unharmed.
The court's decision was to free the accused individual due to insufficient proof.
The activists worked tirelessly to free the wrongfully imprisoned man.

senses.definition: the punishment of killing a criminal, which is officially ordered by a court

examples.x: The court sentenced him to the death penalty.
The debate over the death penalty continues worldwide.
Many countries have abolished the death penalty.
The judge ruled out the death penalty for the case.
The death penalty is rarely used in some states.

senses.definition: related to or involving illegal activities

examples.x: The new law aims to reduce criminal activity by increasing penalties for repeat offenders.
The detective spent years studying criminal behavior to better understand the motives behind various crimes.
The judge handed down a harsh sentence due to the criminal nature of the defendant's actions.
Legal procedures ensure that individuals accused of criminal conduct receive fair trials and due process.

senses.definition: a person who does or is involved in an illegal activity

examples.x: The police arrested the criminal after a long investigation.
The criminal was sentenced to five years in prison.
He became a wanted criminal after escaping from custody.
The detectives worked hard to catch the dangerous criminal.
The criminal confessed to robbing the bank.
